title: Cacalia fruticulosa
taxonomy: Cacalia fruticulosa is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the family Asteraceae. It is a member of the tribe Senecioneae and the genus Cacalia. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and morphology, which are typical of the Asteraceae family. The species is native to the mountainous regions of China and is known for its unique appearance and growth habits. Further research may be necessary to determine the specific classification and relationships within the plant's family and genus.

Its leaves and stems contain compounds with potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Some studies suggest that it may have applications in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions due to its bioactive compounds. However, more research is required to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal plant.
